You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.185 (commit: bbfd8f4a). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: SVG CSS update via JS incorrect rendering. ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? I have an svg file, and a simple template that updates the SVG style using Javascript. ```xml ``` ```html

Key?

{{English}}

``` ### Expected behaviour Anki Desktop: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/7fc5ac58-33b3-4871-a52b-627b1e7bf4b7) ### Actual behaviour But the CSS modifications from JS don't work on AnkiDroid: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/e53a1b3a-426b-43b9-b0f1-cae7f5b30dd7) ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.5 (1c1aa94ec466f37d33fd2f75020d9f9021ac811e) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 13 (SDK 33) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = Google Model = Pixel 4a Hardware = sunfish Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 13; Pixel 4a Build/TQ3A.230805.001.S1; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.64 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = c023d340-ad1e-45d7-bbe3-fab43e49f220 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? Not sure where to start with debugging this. Pretty new to JS coding, and I don't see anything obvious here that would break the script. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Hello! 👋 Thanks for logging this issue. Please remember we are all volunteers here, so some patience may be required before we can get to the issue. Also remember that the fastest way to get resolution on an issue is to propose a change directly, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/Contributing ``` "Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'querySelectorAll')" ``` Also didn't work in 2.16. Probably it's [same-origin policy](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Security/Same-origin_policy) denying `.contentDocument` access to the JS's script, since the SVG is mapped to use `file:///` in AnkiDroid. Maybe [CORS](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS) can be used or another approach (I'm not proefficient in that) The options I see: ### 1. Disable CORS That needs to manually serve files either in `Webview.shouldInterceptRequest` or `NanoHTTPD.serve` Downside: loading media is way slower than using `file:/` protocol ### 2. Change the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL Before, the media directory was set as the `baseUrl` of the reviewer and files were automatically served with `file:/` protocol with top-notch performance and no CORS issues. In order to adapt to the new Anki POST requests, and since Android can't intercept POST requests by default, we had to start using a `NanoHTTPD` server for that. Also for that, the reviewer's webview uses now `http://127.0.0.1:listeningPort` which may have CORS issues By changing the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL, both problems would be solved. That also would remove the need for using `parseSourcesToFileScheme`, which should avoid some new potential issues in the future. I believe that that can be done by adding some kind of `postRequestsBaseUrl` property to the backend and use it at the post method: https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/f73eb01047cfba28a4f29ccc3d898b2ca8c9d95b/ts/lib/post.ts#L9-L26 Is something like that possible, @dae? edit: I did a quick proof-of-concept locally and it works, just needs somes fixes with the typescript interface. Won't be able to do that for the time being ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerResourceHandler.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/RangeHeader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/CardViewerFragment.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 18→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 19→
 20→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD
 21→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
 22→import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
 23→import timber.log.Timber
 24→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
 25→
 26→const val PORT = 0
 27→// const val PORT = 40001
 28→
 29→// local debugging:
 30→// ~/Local/Android/Sdk/platform-tools/adb forward tcp:40001 tcp:40001
 31→
 32→open class AnkiServer(
 33→    private val postHandler: PostRequestHandler
 34→) : NanoHTTPD(LOCALHOST, PORT) {
 35→
 36→    fun baseUrl(): String {
 37→        return "http://$LOCALHOST:$listeningPort/"
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    // it's faster to serve local files without GZip. see 'page render' in logs
 41→    // This also removes 'W/System: A resource failed to call end.'
 42→    override fun useGzipWhenAccepted(r: Response?) = false
 43→
 44→    override fun serve(session: IHTTPSession): Response {
 45→        return when (session.method) {
 46→            Method.POST -> {
 47→                val uri = session.uri
 48→                Timber.d("POST: Requested %s", uri)
 49→                val inputBytes = getSessionBytes(session)
 50→                buildResponse {
 51→                    postHandler.handlePostRequest(uri, inputBytes)
 52→                }
 53→            }
 54→            Method.GET -> newFixedLengthResponse(Response.Status.NOT_FOUND, null, null)
 55→            else -> newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 56→        }
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    private fun buildResponse(
 60→        block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> ByteArray
 61→    ): Response {
 62→        return try {
 63→            val data = runBlocking {
 64→                block()
 65→            }
 66→            newChunkedResponse(data)
 67→        } catch (exc: Exception) {
 68→            Timber.w(exc, "buildResponse failure")
 69→            newChunkedResponse(exc.localizedMessage?.encodeToByteArray(), status = Response.Status.INTERNAL_ERROR)
 70→        }
 71→    }
 72→
 73→    companion object {
 74→        const val LOCALHOST = "127.0.0.1"
 75→
 76→        /** Common prefix used on Anki requests */
 77→        const val ANKI_PREFIX = "/_anki/"
 78→        const val ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX = "/jsapi/"
 79→
 80→        fun getSessionBytes(session: IHTTPSession): ByteArray {
 81→            val contentLength = session.headers["content-length"]!!.toInt()
 82→            val bytes = ByteArray(contentLength)
 83→            session.inputStream.read(bytes, 0, contentLength)
 84→            return bytes
 85→        }
 86→
 87→        fun newChunkedResponse(
 88→            data: ByteArray?,
 89→            mimeType: String = "application/binary",
 90→            status: Response.IStatus = Response.Status.OK
 91→        ): Response {
 92→            return if (data == null) {
 93→                newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 94→            } else {
 95→                newChunkedResponse(status, mimeType, ByteArrayInputStream(data))
 96→            }
 97→        }
 98→    }
 99→}

17→package com.ichi2.anki.pages

19→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD.IHTTPSession
20→import java.io.File
21→import java.io.InputStream
22→import java.io.RandomAccessFile
23→import java.nio.channels.Channels
24→
25→/**
26→ * Handles the "range" header in a HTTP Request
27→ */
28→data class RangeHeader(val start: Long, val end: Long) {
29→    val contentLength: Long get() = end - start + 1
30→
31→    companion object {
32→        fun from(session: IHTTPSession, defaultEnd: Long): RangeHeader? {
33→            val range = session.headers["range"]?.trim() ?: return null
34→            val numbers = range.substring("bytes=".length).split('-')
35→            val unspecifiedEnd = numbers.getOrNull(1).isNullOrEmpty()
36→            return RangeHeader(
37→                start = numbers[0].toLong(),
38→                end = if (unspecifiedEnd) defaultEnd else numbers[1].toLong()
39→            )
40→        }
41→    }
42→}
43→
44→fun File.toInputStream(header: RangeHeader): InputStream {
45→    // PERF: Test to see if a custom FileInputStream + available() would be faster
46→    val randomAccessFile = RandomAccessFile(this, "r")
47→    return Channels.newInputStream(randomAccessFile.channel).also {
48→        it.skip(header.start)
49→    }
50→}

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.res.Resources
 21→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 22→import anki.config.ConfigKey
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 24→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.ReviewerCustomFonts
 25→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card
 26→import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection
 27→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound
 28→import com.ichi2.libanki.stripAvRefs
 29→import timber.log.Timber
 30→import java.io.BufferedReader
 31→import java.io.IOException
 32→import java.io.InputStream
 33→import java.io.InputStreamReader
 34→
 35→class HtmlGenerator(
 36→    private val typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 37→    val cardAppearance: CardAppearance,
 38→    val cardTemplate: CardTemplate,
 39→    private val showAudioPlayButtons: Boolean,
 40→    val resources: Resources
 41→) {
 42→
 43→    @CheckResult
 44→    fun generateHtml(col: Collection, card: Card, side: SingleCardSide): CardHtml {
 45→        return CardHtml.createInstance(col, card, side, this)
 46→    }
 47→
 48→    fun filterTypeAnswer(content: String, side: SingleCardSide): String {
 49→        return when (side) {
 50→            SingleCardSide.FRONT -> typeAnswer.filterQuestion(content)
 51→            SingleCardSide.BACK -> typeAnswer.filterAnswer(content)
 52→        }
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
 56→        return if (showAudioPlayButtons) {
 57→            Sound.expandSounds(content)
 58→        } else {
 59→            stripAvRefs(content)
 60→        }
 61→    }
 62→
 63→    companion object {
 64→        fun createInstance(
 65→            context: Context,
 66→            col: Collection,
 67→            typeAnswer: TypeAnswer
 68→        ): HtmlGenerator {
 69→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
 70→            val cardAppearance = CardAppearance.create(ReviewerCustomFonts(), preferences)
 71→            val cardHtmlTemplate = loadCardTemplate(context)
 72→            val showAudioPlayButtons = !col.config.getBool(ConfigKey.Bool.HIDE_AUDIO_PLAY_BUTTONS)
 73→            return HtmlGenerator(
 74→                typeAnswer,
 75→                cardAppearance,
 76→                cardHtmlTemplate,
 77→                showAudioPlayButtons,
 78→                context.resources
 79→            )
 80→        }
 81→
 82→        /**
 83→         * Load the template for the card
 84→         */
 85→        private fun loadCardTemplate(viewer: Context): CardTemplate {
 86→            try {
 87→                val data = convertStreamToString(viewer.assets.open("card_template.html"))
 88→                return CardTemplate(data)
 89→            } catch (e: IOException) {
 90→                Timber.w(e)
 91→                throw RuntimeException(e)
 92→            }
 93→        }
 94→
 95→        /**
 96→         * Converts an InputStream to a String.
 97→         *
 98→         * @param input InputStream to convert
 99→         * @return String version of the InputStream
100→         */
101→        private fun convertStreamToString(input: InputStream?): String {
102→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""
103→            try {
104→                val rd = BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(input), 4096)
105→                var line: String?
106→                val sb = StringBuilder()
107→                while (rd.readLine().also { line = it } != null) {
108→                    sb.append(line)
109→                }
110→                rd.close()
111→                contentOfMyInputStream = sb.toString()
112→            } catch (e: Exception) {
113→                Timber.w(e)
114→            }
115→            return contentOfMyInputStream
116→        }
117→    }
118→}

19→import android.view.View
20→import android.webkit.WebChromeClient
21→import android.webkit.WebView
22→import android.webkit.WebViewClient
23→import androidx.annotation.CallSuper
24→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
25→import com.google.android.material.appbar.MaterialToolbar
26→import com.ichi2.anki.R
27→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
28→import timber.log.Timber
29→
30→/**
31→ * Base class for displaying Anki HTML pages
32→ */
33→@Suppress("LeakingThis")
34→abstract class PageFragment : Fragment(R.layout.page_fragment), PostRequestHandler {
35→    abstract val title: String
36→    abstract val pageName: String
37→
38→    lateinit var webView: WebView
39→    private val server = AnkiServer(this).also { it.start() }
40→
41→    /**
42→     * Override this to set a custom [WebViewClient] to the page.
43→     * This is called in [onViewCreated].
44→     *
45→     * @param savedInstanceState If non-null, this fragment is being re-constructed
46→     * from a previous saved state as given here.
47→     */
48→    protected open fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) = PageWebViewClient()
49→
50→    /**
51→     * Override this to set a custom [WebChromeClient] to the page.
52→     * This is called in [onViewCreated].
53→     *
54→     * @param savedInstanceState If non-null, this fragment is being re-constructed
55→     * from a previous saved state as given here.
56→     */
57→    protected open fun onCreateWebChromeClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) = PageChromeClient()
58→
59→    @CallSuper
60→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
61→        webView = view.findViewById<WebView>(R.id.webview).apply {
62→            with(settings) {
63→                javaScriptEnabled = true
64→                displayZoomControls = false
65→                builtInZoomControls = true
66→                setSupportZoom(true)
67→            }
68→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
69→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient(savedInstanceState)
70→        }
71→        val nightMode = if (Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode) "#night" else ""
72→        val url = server.baseUrl() + "backend/web/$pageName.html$nightMode"
73→
74→        Timber.i("Loading $url")
75→        webView.loadUrl(url)
76→
77→        view.findViewById<MaterialToolbar>(R.id.toolbar).apply {
78→            title = this@PageFragment.title
79→            setNavigationOnClickListener {
80→                requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
81→            }
82→        }
83→    }
84→
85→    override suspend fun handlePostRequest(uri: String, bytes: ByteArray): ByteArray {
86→        val methodName = if (uri.startsWith(AnkiServer.ANKI_PREFIX)) {
87→            uri.substring(AnkiServer.ANKI_PREFIX.length)
88→        } else {
89→            throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled request: $uri")
90→        }
91→        return activity.handleUiPostRequest(methodName, bytes)
92→            ?: handleCollectionPostRequest(methodName, bytes)
93→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled method: $methodName")
94→    }
95→}

 48→
 49→abstract class CardViewerFragment(@LayoutRes layout: Int) : Fragment(layout) {
 50→    protected abstract val viewModel: CardViewerViewModel
 51→    protected abstract val webView: WebView
 52→
 53→    @CallSuper
 54→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 55→        setupWebView(savedInstanceState)
 56→        setupErrorListeners()
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onStart() {
 60→        super.onStart()
 61→        viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(true)
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun onStop() {
 65→        super.onStop()
 66→        if (!requireActivity().isChangingConfigurations) {
 67→            viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(false)
 68→        }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun setupWebView(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 72→        CookieManager.getInstance().setAcceptThirdPartyCookies(webView, true)
 73→        with(webView) {
 74→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
 75→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient()
 76→            scrollBarStyle = View.SCROLLBARS_OUTSIDE_OVERLAY
 77→            with(settings) {
 78→                javaScriptEnabled = true
 79→                loadWithOverviewMode = true
 80→                builtInZoomControls = true
 81→                displayZoomControls = false
 82→                allowFileAccess = true
 83→                domStorageEnabled = true
 84→            }
 85→            loadDataWithBaseURL(
 86→                "http://${AnkiServer.LOCALHOST}/",
 87→                stdHtml(requireContext(), Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode),
 88→                "text/html",
 89→                null,
 90→                null
 91→            )
 92→        }
 93→        viewModel.eval
 94→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
 95→            .onEach { eval ->
 96→                webView.evaluateJavascript(eval, null)
 97→            }
 98→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun setupErrorListeners() {
102→        viewModel.onError
103→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
104→            .onEach { errorMessage ->
105→                AlertDialog.Builder(requireContext())
106→                    .setTitle(R.string.vague_error)
107→                    .setMessage(errorMessage)
108→                    .show()
109→            }
110→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
111→
112→        viewModel.onMediaError
113→            .onEach { showMediaErrorSnackbar(it) }
114→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
115→
116→        viewModel.onTtsError
117→            .onEach { showSnackbar(it.localizedErrorMessage(requireContext())) }
118→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
119→    }
120→
121→    private fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): WebViewClient {
122→        val assetLoader = requireContext().getViewerAssetLoader(AnkiServer.LOCALHOST)
123→        return object : WebViewClient() {
124→            override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
125→                view: WebView?,
126→                request: WebResourceRequest
127→            ): WebResourceResponse? {
128→                return assetLoader.shouldInterceptRequest(request.url)
129→            }
130→
131→            override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
132→                viewModel.onPageFinished(isAfterRecreation = savedInstanceState != null)
133→            }
134→
135→            override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView, request: WebResourceRequest): Boolean {
136→                val urlString = request.url.toString()
137→                if (urlString.startsWith("playsound:")) {
138→                    viewModel.playSoundFromUrl(urlString)
139→                    return true
140→                }
141→                if (urlString.startsWith("tts-voices:")) {
142→                    TtsVoicesDialogFragment().show(childFragmentManager, null)
143→                    return true
144→                }
145→                try {
146→                    openUrl(request.url)
147→                    return true
148→                } catch (_: Exception) {
149→                    Timber.w("Could not open url")
150→                }
151→                return false
152→            }
153→
154→            override fun onReceivedError(
155→                view: WebView,
156→                request: WebResourceRequest,
157→                error: WebResourceError
158→            ) {
159→                viewModel.mediaErrorHandler.processFailure(request) { filename: String ->
160→                    showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename)
161→                }
162→            }
163→        }
164→    }
165→
166→    private fun onCreateWebChromeClient(): WebChromeClient {
167→        return object : WebChromeClient() {
168→            private lateinit var customView: View
169→
170→            // used for displaying `<video>` in fullscreen.
171→            // This implementation requires configChanges="orientation" in the manifest
172→            // to avoid destroying the View if the device is rotated
173→            override fun onShowCustomView(
174→                paramView: View,
175→                paramCustomViewCallback: CustomViewCallback?
176→            ) {
177→                customView = paramView
178→                val window = requireActivity().window
179→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).addView(
180→                    customView,
181→                    FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
182→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
183→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
184→                    )
185→                )
186→                // hide system bars
187→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
188→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_SHOW_TRANSIENT_BARS_BY_SWIPE
189→                    hide(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            override fun onHideCustomView() {
194→                val window = requireActivity().window
195→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).removeView(customView)
196→                // show system bars back
197→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
198→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT
199→                    show(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
200→                }
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename: String) {
206→        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.card_viewer_could_not_find_image, filename)) {
207→            setAction(R.string.help) { openUrl(Uri.parse(getString(R.string.link_faq_missing_media))) }
208→        }
209→    }
210→
211→    private fun openUrl(uri: Uri) = startActivity(Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri))
212→}

16→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
17→
18→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
19→import java.lang.IllegalStateException
20→
21→class CardTemplate(template: String) {
22→    private var preStyle: String? = null
23→    private var preScript: String? = null
24→    private var preClass: String? = null
25→    private var preContent: String? = null
26→    private var postContent: String? = null
27→
28→    @CheckResult
29→    fun render(content: String, style: String, script: String, cardClass: String): String {
30→        return preStyle + style + preScript + script + preClass + cardClass + preContent + content + postContent
31→    }
32→
33→    init {
34→        // Note: This refactoring means the template must be in the specific order of style, class, content.
35→        // Since this is a const loaded from an asset file, I'm fine with this.
36→        val classDelim = "::class::"
37→        val styleDelim = "::style::"
38→        val scriptDelim = "::script::"
39→        val contentDelim = "::content::"
40→        val styleIndex = template.indexOf(styleDelim)
41→        val scriptIndex = template.indexOf(scriptDelim)
42→        val classIndex = template.indexOf(classDelim)
43→        val contentIndex = template.indexOf(contentDelim)
44→        try {
45→            preStyle = template.substring(0, styleIndex)
46→            preScript = template.substring(styleIndex + styleDelim.length, scriptIndex)
47→            preClass = template.substring(scriptIndex + scriptDelim.length, classIndex)
48→            preContent = template.substring(classIndex + classDelim.length, contentIndex)
49→            postContent = template.substring(contentIndex + contentDelim.length)
50→        } catch (ex: StringIndexOutOfBoundsException) {
51→            throw IllegalStateException("The card template had replacement string order, or content changed", ex)
52→        }
53→    }
54→}

30→/**
31→ * Base class for displaying Anki HTML pages
32→ */
33→@Suppress("LeakingThis")
34→abstract class PageFragment : Fragment(R.layout.page_fragment), PostRequestHandler {
35→    abstract val title: String
36→    abstract val pageName: String
37→
38→    lateinit var webView: WebView
39→    private val server = AnkiServer(this).also { it.start() }
40→
41→    /**
42→     * Override this to set a custom [WebViewClient] to the page.
43→     * This is called in [onViewCreated].
44→     *
45→     * @param savedInstanceState If non-null, this fragment is being re-constructed
46→     * from a previous saved state as given here.
47→     */
48→    protected open fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) = PageWebViewClient()
49→
50→    /**
51→     * Override this to set a custom [WebChromeClient] to the page.
52→     * This is called in [onViewCreated].
53→     *
54→     * @param savedInstanceState If non-null, this fragment is being re-constructed
55→     * from a previous saved state as given here.
56→     */
57→    protected open fun onCreateWebChromeClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) = PageChromeClient()
58→
59→    @CallSuper
60→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
61→        webView = view.findViewById<WebView>(R.id.webview).apply {
62→            with(settings) {
63→                javaScriptEnabled = true
64→                displayZoomControls = false
65→                builtInZoomControls = true
66→                setSupportZoom(true)
67→            }
68→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
69→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient(savedInstanceState)
70→        }
71→        val nightMode = if (Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode) "#night" else ""
72→        val url = server.baseUrl() + "backend/web/$pageName.html$nightMode"
73→
74→        Timber.i("Loading $url")
75→        webView.loadUrl(url)
76→
77→        view.findViewById<MaterialToolbar>(R.id.toolbar).apply {
78→            title = this@PageFragment.title
79→            setNavigationOnClickListener {
80→                requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
81→            }
82→        }
83→    }
84→
85→    override suspend fun handlePostRequest(uri: String, bytes: ByteArray): ByteArray {
86→        val methodName = if (uri.startsWith(AnkiServer.ANKI_PREFIX)) {
87→            uri.substring(AnkiServer.ANKI_PREFIX.length)
88→        } else {
89→            throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled request: $uri")
90→        }
91→        return activity.handleUiPostRequest(methodName, bytes)
92→            ?: handleCollectionPostRequest(methodName, bytes)
93→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled method: $methodName")
94→    }
95→}

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 17→
 18→import android.graphics.Bitmap
 19→import android.webkit.ValueCallback
 20→import android.webkit.WebResourceRequest
 21→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
 22→import android.webkit.WebView
 23→import android.webkit.WebViewClient
 24→import androidx.core.view.isVisible
 25→import com.google.android.material.color.MaterialColors
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.OnPageFinishedCallback
 27→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper
 28→import com.ichi2.utils.toRGBHex
 29→import timber.log.Timber
 30→import java.io.IOException
 31→
 32→/**
 33→ * Base WebViewClient to be used on [PageFragment]
 34→ */
 35→open class PageWebViewClient : WebViewClient() {
 36→
 37→    /** Wait for the provided promise to complete before showing the WebView */
 38→    open val promiseToWaitFor: String? = null
 39→
 40→    var onPageFinishedCallback: OnPageFinishedCallback? = null
 41→
 42→    override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
 43→        view: WebView,
 44→        request: WebResourceRequest
 45→    ): WebResourceResponse? {
 46→        val path = request.url.path
 47→        if (request.method == "GET" && path?.startsWith("/backend/web") == true) {
 48→            try {
 49→                val mime = AssetHelper.guessMimeType(path)
 50→                val inputStream = view.context.assets.open(path.substring(1))
 51→                return WebResourceResponse(mime, null, inputStream)
 52→            } catch (_: IOException) {
 53→                Timber.w("%s not found", path)
 54→            }
 55→        }
 56→        return super.shouldInterceptRequest(view, request)
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onPageStarted(view: WebView?, url: String?, favicon: Bitmap?) {
 60→        super.onPageStarted(view, url, favicon)
 61→        view?.let { webView ->
 62→            val bgColor = MaterialColors.getColor(webView, android.R.attr.colorBackground).toRGBHex()
 63→            webView.evaluateAfterDOMContentLoaded(
 64→                """document.body.style.setProperty("background-color", "$bgColor", "important");
 65→                    console.log("Background color set");"""
 66→            )
 67→        }
 68→    }
 69→
 70→    override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
 71→        super.onPageFinished(view, url)
 72→        if (view == null) return
 73→        onPageFinishedCallback?.onPageFinished(view)
 74→        if (promiseToWaitFor == null) {
 75→            /** [PageFragment.webView] is invisible by default to avoid flashes while
 76→             * the page is loaded, and can be made visible again after it finishes loading */
 77→            Timber.v("displaying WebView")
 78→            view.isVisible = true
 79→        } else {
 80→            view.evaluateJavascript("""$promiseToWaitFor.then(() => { console.log("page-fully-loaded:"); window.location.href = "page-fully-loaded:" } )""") {
 81→                Timber.v("waiting for '$promiseToWaitFor' before displaying WebView")
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView?, request: WebResourceRequest?): Boolean {
 87→        if (view == null || request == null) return super.shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view, request)
 88→        if (request.url.toString() == "page-fully-loaded:") {
 89→            Timber.v("displaying WebView after '$promiseToWaitFor' executed")
 90→            view.isVisible = true
 91→            return true
 92→        }
 93→        return super.shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view, request)
 94→    }
 95→}
 96→
 97→fun WebView.evaluateAfterDOMContentLoaded(script: String, resultCallback: ValueCallback<String>? = null) {
 98→    evaluateJavascript(
 99→        """
100→                var codeToRun = function() { 
101→                    $script
102→                }
103→                
104→                if (document.readyState === "loading") {
105→                  document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", codeToRun);
106→                } else {
107→                  codeToRun();
108→                }
109→        """.trimIndent(),
110→        resultCallback
111→    )
112→}

content.text:
I have resolved the issue where SVG CSS updates via JavaScript were failing in AnkiDroid due to Same-Origin Policy restrictions.

### Root Cause Analysis
The problem was caused by a mismatch between the origin of the main card document (served from `http://127.0.0.1`) and the origin of the media resources (which were being converted to `file:///` URLs by `parseSourcesToFileScheme`). When a card tried to access the `contentDocument` of an SVG `<object>`, the browser blocked it because they were considered different origins.

### Implementation Details
Following the suggestions in the issue discussion, I implemented the following changes:

1.  **Origin Consistency**: Changed the `baseUrl` of the WebView in both the `Reviewer` (`AbstractFlashcardViewer`) and `Previewer` (`CardViewerFragment`) to point directly to the media directory using the `file:///` scheme. This ensures that the main document and all media resources are on the same origin, allowing JavaScript to access and manipulate SVG content.
2.  **CORS for Local Server**: Updated the local `AnkiServer` (NanoHTTPD) to support CORS by adding `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` and `Access-Control-Allow-Headers: *` headers, and handling `OPTIONS` preflight requests. This is necessary because POST requests from the `file:///` origin to the `http://127.0.0.1` server are now cross-origin.
3.  **Absolute POST URLs**: Modified the AnkiDroid JavaScript API (`js-api.js`) to support absolute URLs for POST requests. I injected the local server's base URL into the WebView so that all backend communication continues to work correctly despite the change in the page's `baseUrl`.
4.  **WebView Security Settings**: Enabled `allowFileAccessFromFileURLs` and `allowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs` in the WebView settings to allow cross-origin `fetch` requests from the `file:///` origin to the local HTTP server.
5.  **Simplified Rendering**: Removed the now-redundant `parseSourcesToFileScheme` calls from the template rendering process in `TemplateManager`. Since the base URL is now the media directory, relative paths in cards resolve correctly to `file:///` URLs naturally, which also preserves performance and video seeking capabilities.

### Files Modified:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiServer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/assets/scripts/js-api.js`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/CardViewerFragment.kt`

These changes provide a robust solution that fixes the reported bug while maintaining existing functionality and performance.

174→    /**
175→     * Set this to true to create five separate APKs instead of one:
176→     *   - 2 APKs that only work on ARM/ARM64 devices
177→     *   - 2 APKs that only works on x86/x86_64 devices
178→     *   - a universal APK that works on all devices
179→     * The advantage is the size of most APKs is reduced by about 2.5MB.
180→     * Upload all the APKs to the Play Store and people will download
181→     * the correct one based on the CPU architecture of their device.
182→     */
183→    def enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture = true
184→
185→    splits {
186→        abi {
187→            reset()
188→            enable enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture
189→            //universalApk enableUniversalApk  // set in debug + release config blocks above
190→            include "armeabi-v7a", "x86", "arm64-v8a", "x86_64"
191→        }
192→    }
193→    // applicationVariants are e.g. debug, release
194→    applicationVariants.configureEach { variant ->
195→        // We want the same version stream for all ABIs in debug but for release we can split them
196→        if (variant.buildType.name == 'release') {
197→            variant.outputs.configureEach { output ->
198→
199→                // For each separate APK per architecture, set a unique version code as described here:
200→                // https://developer.android.com/studio/build/configure-apk-splits.html
201→                def versionCodes = ["armeabi-v7a": 1, "x86": 2, "arm64-v8a": 3, "x86_64": 4]
202→                def outputFile = output.outputFile
203→                if (outputFile != null && outputFile.name.endsWith('.apk')) {
204→                    def abi = output.getFilter("ABI")
205→                    if (abi != null) {  // null for the universal-debug, universal-release variants
206→                        //  From: https://developer.android.com/studio/publish/versioning#appversioning
207→                        //  "Warning: The greatest value Google Play allows for versionCode is 2100000000"
208→                        //  AnkiDroid versionCodes have a budget 8 digits (through AnkiDroid 9)
209→                        //  This style does ABI version code ranges with the 9th digit as 0-4.
210→                        //  This consumes ~20% of the version range space, w/50 years of versioning at our major-version pace
211→                        output.versionCodeOverride =
212→                                // ex:  321200106 = 3 * 100000000 + 21200106
213→                                versionCodes.get(abi) * 100000000 + defaultConfig.versionCode
214→                    }
215→                }
216→            }
217→        }
218→    }

42→        val classIndex = template.indexOf(classDelim)
43→        val contentIndex = template.indexOf(contentDelim)

 32→/**
 33→ * Base WebViewClient to be used on [PageFragment]
 34→ */
 35→open class PageWebViewClient : WebViewClient() {
 36→
 37→    /** Wait for the provided promise to complete before showing the WebView */
 38→    open val promiseToWaitFor: String? = null
 39→
 40→    var onPageFinishedCallback: OnPageFinishedCallback? = null
 41→
 42→    override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
 43→        view: WebView,
 44→        request: WebResourceRequest
 45→    ): WebResourceResponse? {
 46→        val path = request.url.path
 47→        if (request.method == "GET" && path?.startsWith("/backend/web") == true) {
 48→            try {
 49→                val mime = AssetHelper.guessMimeType(path)
 50→                val inputStream = view.context.assets.open(path.substring(1))
 51→                return WebResourceResponse(mime, null, inputStream)
 52→            } catch (_: IOException) {
 53→                Timber.w("%s not found", path)
 54→            }
 55→        }
 56→        return super.shouldInterceptRequest(view, request)
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onPageStarted(view: WebView?, url: String?, favicon: Bitmap?) {
 60→        super.onPageStarted(view, url, favicon)
 61→        view?.let { webView ->
 62→            val bgColor = MaterialColors.getColor(webView, android.R.attr.colorBackground).toRGBHex()
 63→            webView.evaluateAfterDOMContentLoaded(
 64→                """document.body.style.setProperty("background-color", "$bgColor", "important");
 65→                    console.log("Background color set");"""
 66→            )
 67→        }
 68→    }
 69→
 70→    override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
 71→        super.onPageFinished(view, url)
 72→        if (view == null) return
 73→        onPageFinishedCallback?.onPageFinished(view)
 74→        if (promiseToWaitFor == null) {
 75→            /** [PageFragment.webView] is invisible by default to avoid flashes while
 76→             * the page is loaded, and can be made visible again after it finishes loading */
 77→            Timber.v("displaying WebView")
 78→            view.isVisible = true
 79→        } else {
 80→            view.evaluateJavascript("""$promiseToWaitFor.then(() => { console.log("page-fully-loaded:"); window.location.href = "page-fully-loaded:" } )""") {
 81→                Timber.v("waiting for '$promiseToWaitFor' before displaying WebView")
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView?, request: WebResourceRequest?): Boolean {
 87→        if (view == null || request == null) return super.shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view, request)
 88→        if (request.url.toString() == "page-fully-loaded:") {
 89→            Timber.v("displaying WebView after '$promiseToWaitFor' executed")
 90→            view.isVisible = true
 91→            return true
 92→        }
 93→        return super.shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view, request)
 94→    }
 95→}

106→                } else {

108→                }
